Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 4, 160 [Enuma Anu Enlil Varia][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ⸢AN⸣.GE₆ GARun ina EN.NUN.AN.[USAN₂ ...]

(o 1) [...] an eclipse takes place during the evening watch (and) rises [...]: in his land, well-being. The harvest ... [...]. The land will be sated with rain.

o 22

[...] iš-ḫu-uṭ ina KUR-šu SILIM BURU₁₄ x [...]

o 33

[...] KUR ša₂-me i-[šeb-bi]

o 44

[... AN].⸢GE₆⸣ GARun ina EN.NUN.MURU₂.BA a-na tu₁₅⸢KUR⸣.[RA ...]

(o 4) [...] an eclipse takes place in the middle watch [...] towards the east: [...] will be numerous. The land will be sated with rain.

o 55

[...] i-ma-a-du KUR ša₂-me i-⸢šeb⸣-[bi]

o 66

[... GAR]un ina EN.NUN.U₄.ZAL.LI a-na tu₁₅SI.⸢SA₂⸣ [...]

(o 6) [...] takes place in the dawn watch [...] towards the north: [...] will be numerous. The land will be sated with rain.

o 77

[...] ⸢i⸣-ma-a-du KUR ša₂-me i-šeb-bi

o 88

[...] ITI KUR-MEŠ

(o 8) [...] month of the lands.

o 99

[...] ⸢EN.NUN.AN.USAN₂⸣ a-na tu₁₅U₁₈.LU iš-ḫu-uṭ

(o 9) [...] evening watch (and) rises towards the south: [...]. The land [...] rain.

o 1010

[...] KUR ša₂-me [...]

o 1111

[...] ⸢iš⸣-ḫu-[uṭ ...]

(o 11) [...] rises [...].

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] x x x [...]

(r 1') [...] ... [...].

r 2'2'

[... EN.NUN.U₄].⸢ZAL⸣.LA a-na tu₁₅SI.⸢SA₂⸣ [...]

(r 2') [...] dawn watch [...] towards the north: [...] ... his property, that man ... [...].

r 3'3'

[...] x UN DI bu-ša₂-a-šu₂ LU₂ BI x [...]

r 4'4'

[... EN].NUN.AN.USAN₂ a-na tu₁₅⸢U₁₈⸣.LU iš-⸢ḫu⸣-[uṭ]

(r 4') [...] evening watch (and) rises towards the south: [...]. Adad will devastate the harvest.

r 5'5'

[...] dIŠKUR BURU₁₄ RA⸢iṣ⸣

r 6'6'

[...] ⸢ina⸣ EN.NUN.MURU₂.BA ana tu₁₅KUR.RA iš-ḫu-uṭ

(r 6') [...] during the middle watch (and) rises towards the east: [...] .... Adad will devastate the harvest.

r 7'7'

[... x]-lit-tu dIŠKUR BURU₁₄ RA⸢iṣ⸣

r 8'8'

[... GAR]⸢un⸣ ina EN.NUN.U₄.ZAL.LA a-⸢na⸣ tu₁₅SI.SA₂ iš-ḫu-uṭ

(r 8') [...] takes place during the dawn watch and rises towards the north: [...] .... Adad will devastate the harvest.

r 9'9'

[...] x-lit-tu dIŠKUR BURU₁₄ RAiṣ

r 10'10'

[... x]-KAM AN.GE₆ <ina> EN.NUN.AN.USAN₂ d30 x [...]

(r 10') [...] an eclipse during the evening watch, the Moon ... [...].

r 11'11'

[...] GIŠ US U₅ab NA₄ ina [...]

(r 11') [...] rides ...: a stone in [...].1

1 Presumably this is a catch-line.
